creator: jhb@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Thursday 25 January 2007 04:29, Armin Mohring wrote:
> During boot, the AGP graphics card is recognized as ISA graphics card.
> As a result, the kernel crashes and causes an core dump.

FreeBSD normally sees the ISA-compat part of VGA cards as one of the last 
printfs during boot.  That is normal.  What we need are the actual crash 
messages.  If you can setup a serial console to capture a verbose boot that 
would be ideal.

-- 
John Baldwin
